rationales.self_direction: Even with five free hours, she says sitting with a novel felt like neglecting her job and lets household admin dictate the afternoon instead of choosing what she wants.
rationales.hedonism: She cuts short a rare pocket of pleasure by folding laundry and doing non-urgent school admin, then says she can only justify enjoyment if it produces a visible result.
rationales.conformity: She treats being idle in the house as failing at her role and defaults to the expected domestic tasks even when no one is home and nothing is urgent.

rationales.self_direction: She keeps skipping the one activity that is only for herself and says wanting something purely because she wants it feels like a system error.
rationales.hedonism: Ceramics is her one outlet unrelated to the boys or the house, yet she repeatedly denies herself that enjoyable time and stays home instead.
rationales.conformity: She says the class would feel justified only if Pieter explicitly released her or if it produced something useful for someone else, showing strong deference to role expectations.

rationales.hedonism: She says yes to fries because why not, sits by the canal, and notes that the fries were excellent, choosing a small pleasurable break over immediate household efficiency.
rationales.benevolence: She follows Mees's request for fries and lingers on the shared laugh about mayonnaise on his nose, centering a warm moment with her son.

rationales.self_direction: With Pieter home and the boys fed, nothing external blocks her, yet she spends the ceramics slot scrolling and says the only person keeping her on the sofa is herself.
rationales.hedonism: She misses ceramics again despite wanting to go, comparing it to refusing the dish you actually want and settling for the bland option.
